title: Post-cardiology admission follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.
assessment.rationale: NG185 supports discharge-summary, rehabilitation, symptom-change, question, communication, and follow-up documentation when the cardiology admission involved acute coronary syndrome; it does not generalise that diagnosis to every admission.

source.title: Acute coronary syndromes
source.population: Adults with acute coronary syndromes, including people treated with percutaneous coronary intervention after myocardial infarction.
source.setting: Acute cardiology, discharge, cardiac rehabilitation, and post-myocardial-infarction follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for post-acute-coronary-syndrome and post-myocardial-infarction PCI care; partial for a generic post-PCI workflow because not every PCI follows acute coronary syndrome.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.34 — discharge information and cardiac rehabilitation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

assessment.text: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.
assessment.rationale: Heart-failure care-plan review was checked as a conditional alternative admission context and was not used to infer heart failure.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected or established chronic heart failure.
source.setting: Primary, community, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic-heart-failure diagnosis, review, monitoring, and clinician-led management; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4–1.1.9 — primary-care review, record, care plan, and access

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

sources_used.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management

additions.final_wording: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.
